field.label: Run As User
field.help: System user used to run AdGuard Home. Defaults to the dedicated, unprivileged "adguardhome" account (least privilege). FIRST-TIME SETUP: AdGuard Home's very first launch must run as "root" (on FreeBSD it hard-requires uid 0 until a config exists) — set this to "root", Save, complete the setup wizard at the web UI (choose a high DNS port like 5353, not 53), then set this back to "adguardhome" and Save to run unprivileged. See the plugin README.

field.label: Configuration File
field.help: Path to AdGuardHome.yaml. AdGuard Home owns and rewrites this file itself; the plugin only points the daemon at it. Change only if you know what you are doing.

field.label: Working Directory
field.help: Directory for AdGuard Home runtime data (filters, query log, statistics, sessions database).

field.label: Web Interface Port
field.help: Port used only to build the "Open AdGuard Home" link below. It does not control which port AdGuard Home listens on — that is configured inside AdGuard Home itself (the initial setup wizard runs on port 3000).

field.label: Extra Options
field.help: Additional command-line arguments passed to the AdGuard Home binary.
